page: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features 45 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9454 processor running at 2.75 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 180.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB per core. For acceleration, the system is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng testing. Memory bandwidth is strong, ranking in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations. The server demonstrates high capability in LLM inference, achieving top-tier (top 10%) speeds for prompt processing and text generation on small (135M) and medium (7B) models, as well as top-tier prompt processing on large (70B) models. Multi-threaded decompression and ray tracing benchmarks also rank in the top 10% and top 25% respectively.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs offers a balanced hardware profile for compute-intensive and graphics-accelerated tasks without the overhead of larger multi-GPU nodes. The 400 GB NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, though capacity is modest relative to the high compute and memory specifications. This server is configured for workloads such as large language model inference, ray tracing, multi-threaded data decompression, and parallel compute tasks.
description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 45 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores operating at 2.75 GHz, 180.0 GB of system memory, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. The system is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. In benchmark evaluations, the server achieves top-tier rankings in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and ray tracing capabilities. This hardware profile is designed for parallel computing, large language model inference, and graphics-intensive workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 45 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 180 GB RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM for high-compute and AI inference workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 45 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 180 GB RAM, dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM for accelerated parallel processing.
45 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores running at 2.75 GHz.
180.0 GB of system memory paired with a 400 GB NVMe SSD.
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and LLM inference tasks.
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM hypervisor virtualization.
